name: LLM Reasoning Representation Structure Evaluation Dataset
description:
This dataset contains large language model (LLM) responses to 20 classical cognitive and behavioral experiments, along with structured self-reports and multi-dimensional evaluation scores generated by multiple LLMs. Each experiment consists of multiple prompt variations by demand level across different dimensions. Each prompt is assigned a unique prompt_id that is consistent across all models and datasets.

For each prompt, 7 different LLMs generated responses under a standardized instruction protocol requiring structured JSON outputs including response, reasoning trace, confidence, and experiment identification. Latency (recorded_rt) is measured externally, while asked_rt is self-reported by the model.

Separately, each prompt was evaluated by the same set of 7 LLMs across 20 dimensions derived from a detailed rubric. Each rating is on a scale from 0 to 5. The rating dimensions span multiple domains including Attention and Scan (AS), Comprehension and Expression (CE), Conceptualisation and Abstraction (CL), Metacognition (MC), Mind Modelling (MS), Quantitative and Logical Reasoning (QL), Spatial Reasoning (SN), and Domain Knowledge (KN).

The rating rubric definitions and levels are provided in an accompanying PDF document. :contentReference[oaicite:0]{index=0}

distribution.description: All response CSV files organized as output_results/{model}/{experiment}.csv
All ratings CSV files organized as ratings/{experiment}_ratings_all.csv
Detailed rubric describing the 20 evaluation dimensions and scoring criteria

recordSet.name: responses
recordSet.description:
Each row corresponds to a single LLM response to a prompt instance within a specific experiment. Files are partitioned by model and experiment. Model identity is inferred from the folder name (mini, nano, gpt5, grok, flash, sonnet, haiku). Experiment identity is encoded in the filename.

Each row captures both behavioral output and meta-cognitive self-reports generated by the LLM under strict structured instructions.

field.name: prompt_id
field.description: Unique identifier for a prompt. Format: {experiment_shortname}_{index} (e.g., dgrat_1). Shared across all models and datasets.

recordSet.name: ratings
recordSet.description:
Each row corresponds to a prompt_id and contains evaluation scores assigned by multiple LLMs across 20 cognitive dimensions. Each dimension is scored from 0 to 5 based on a structured rubric. :contentReference[oaicite:1]{index=1}

Column naming convention: {provider}_{model}_{dimension}_score

Examples:
- openai_gpt-5-mini_OBj_score
- anthropic_claude-sonnet-4-5_AS_score
- xai_grok-4.20-reasoning_MCu_score

Where:
- provider: API provider (openai, anthropic, xai, gemini)
- model: model identifier
- dimension: cognitive dimension (e.g., AS, CEc, CEe, CL, MCt, MCu, MCr, MS, QLq, QLl, SNs, KNn, KNs, etc.)
